chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.D-73 of 2026 (Riaz Ali Buriro v. Province of Sindh and others) Date of Hearing Order with the signature of Judge. Before:- Mr. Justice Amjad Ali Bohio, Mr. Justice Ali Haider ‘Ada’. Fresh Case 1. For orders on CMA No.1620/2026 (U/A). 2. For orders on CMA No.183/2026 (U/A). 3. For orders on office objections. 4. For orders on CMA No.184/2026 (Ex/A). 5. For the hearing of the main case. 04.03.2026. Mr. Shahabaz Hafeez Shahani, Advocate for the Petitioner ****************** 1. Granted 2. Dismissed as being infructuous. 3 to 5. The petitioner's grievance is that the revenue hierarchy be directed to demarcate the land and declare its boundaries, as the land allegedly belongs to him. It is contended that he had moved an application before the Mukhtiarkar (Revenue), Rohri, but the same has not yet been decided; therefore, he has filed the instant petition. It has come to the notice of the Revenue Department, Government of Sindh, vide Notification No. DD (E&I) / HQ / BOR / 2024 / 302, Hyderabad, dated 21.10.2024, has established a Public Grievance Redressal Cell to address revenue-related matters through a one-window facility. Under this mechanism, complaints relating to mutation, entries, sale certificates, Foti Khata/badal, demarcation of land, partition of land, solvency certificates, etc., are to be recorded in a complaint register and disposed of on an expedited basis. The Deputy Commissioner of each 2 district has been designated as the Principal Officer for Public Grievance Redressal, and the mechanism functions under his supervision. During the course of arguments, learned counsel for the petitioner candidly conceded that the petitioner has neither filed any complaint nor approached the said Public Grievance Cell.

The Deputy Commissioner of each 2 district has been designated as the Principal Officer for Public Grievance Redressal, and the mechanism functions under his supervision. During the course of arguments, learned counsel for the petitioner candidly conceded that the petitioner has neither filed any complaint nor approached the said Public Grievance Cell. It is well settled that where an adequate and efficacious alternate remedy is available, the constitutional jurisdiction is to be exercised sparingly, and ordinarily a petition is not maintainable in such circumstances. In view of the foregoing facts and circumstances, this petition is hereby dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE
